vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-53550
vulnerability.description: Quadratic-complexity DoS in js-yaml merge key handling via repeated aliases (CWE-407). CVSS v3.1 5.3 (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:L).

statements.impact_statement: js-yaml 3.14.2 is a transitive build-time dependency of the Docusaurus documentation toolchain at docs/docusaurus/ (not listed in docs/docusaurus/package.json direct dependencies). The merge-key DoS vector requires attacker-controlled YAML input. All YAML processed during the Docusaurus build is author-controlled repository content (markdown frontmatter, configuration files) protected by pull-request review gates. No network-accessible surface exposes untrusted YAML to the js-yaml parse path.
statements.status_notes: Reachability analysis: js-yaml parses YAML only during CI documentation builds. Docusaurus is not deployed as a runtime service. The CVSS v3.1 AV:N vector does not apply because there is no network path by which an attacker can supply crafted YAML to the build process without first having a PR merged by a human reviewer. Advisory: https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-h67p-54hq-rp68

vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-48522
vulnerability.description: PyJWKClient accepts non-HTTP(S) URI schemes (file://, ftp://, data:), enabling SSRF and potential token forgery when jku URL is attacker-influenced (CWE-441, CWE-918). CVSS v3.1 4.2 (AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:L/I:L/A:N).

statements.impact_statement: pyjwt 2.12.1 is a transitive dependency of azure-identity within the experimental tts-voiceover skill (.github/skills/experimental/tts-voiceover/uv.lock). The vulnerable class PyJWKClient is never instantiated in any tts-voiceover script. The skill uses azure-identity solely for Azure Speech SDK credential acquisition and does not perform JWT signature verification via user-supplied JWKS URLs. The attack path (attacker-influenced jku URL passed to PyJWKClient) has no execute path in this codebase.
statements.status_notes: Confirmed by inspection of generate_voiceover.py and embed_audio.py: no import of jwt or PyJWKClient. pyjwt is absent from pyproject.toml direct dependencies. azure-identity acquires tokens from Microsoft Entra ID endpoints over HTTPS, not from caller-supplied JWKS URLs. Advisory: https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-993g-76c3-p5m4
